architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.decision: Universal Office should disclose liturgical composition, not merely render prayer text. Composition maps are accepted as a core liturgical-assembly disclosure model. Ribbon placement in the four-volume LOTH is accepted as source-navigation metadata and as a physical-book expression of liturgical composition.

Universal Office must support both prayer-forward and understanding-forward users. Some users enter the Office by praying first and inspecting structure only when needed. Others need the structure explained before they can pray with trust. The app must not rank either posture as spiritually superior.
architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.implication: Composition-map access should be available early enough to serve users for whom understanding is the vestibule of prayer, not merely as a secondary help feature.

architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.decision: The first public audience is English-speaking users, many of whom may not be organically formed inside a single inherited office tradition. The app should assume many users are converts, returnees, mixed-background Christians, clergy-adjacent learners, or autodidacts who need formation-aware structure as well as accurate rendered text.

Communities such as r/divineoffice may be used as discerning product intelligence, not as theological or liturgical authority. Their value is in surfacing recurring real-user friction around ribbons, page navigation, ordinary/propers, app/book coordination, chant markings, and confidence in what is being prayed.

Ribbons, tabs, cards, page markers, and inside-cover material are physical expressions of liturgical state management. Universal Office should translate these affordances into digital composition and navigation models rather than treating them as quaint print artifacts.

Composition-map implementation must proceed governance/schema-first. Do not implement public composition-map UI yet. Next implementation belongs in the private JWJeffery/LOTH source-governance layer with sourceNavigation/ribbonPlacement, then resolver-emitted compositionMap, then diagnostics/internal inspection, and only later PrayerAppNew public UI.

The mobile app should be design-forward rather than Anglican-forward: a digital breviary that opens like a chapel. The visual language should be vigil-dark, text-first, rubrically precise, quietly illuminated, and immersive enough to evoke incense, monastery, cathedral, and Easter Vigil beauty without becoming cartoonish, AI sacred slop, or decorative high-church aestheticism.
architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.governance_rule: Beauty must arise from liturgical function, theological meaning, ecclesial memory, or the office's own structure. It must not be applied as generic sacred decor.

architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.decision: The Universal Office is Anglican-rooted and Anglican-governed, but not publicly self-conscious or defensive about that fact. Anglicanism is the default grammar and fallback tradition, not a denominational banner at the door. The app lives the claim first, explains it when asked, and defends it only when necessary.
architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.theological_formula: Anglicanism within fullness, receiving and learning from the whole.

architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.decision: The app receives the user and asks only what is needed. Onboarding begins with: 'The Church's daily prayer, appointed for the hour. Begin where you pray, and let the office lead you.' First routing asks 'Where do you pray?' with Western Christian, Eastern Christian, and I'm not sure. Branch screens use 'Select your tradition' rather than 'begin with' language.

routing.unsure: Only after the user selects I'm not sure, route quietly to the Anglican Daily Office and explain that it is a stable Western rule of prayer rooted in Scripture, psalmody, and the ancient hours.

architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.decision: First select broad Christian family, then primary tradition, then internal office/use only when multiple governed forms exist. Do not expose fake branch points. Anglican currently routes to TEC 1979 BCP; future ACNA 2019 belongs inside Anglican. Oriental Orthodox currently routes to Ethiopian; future Coptic Agpeya, Armenian, West Syriac / Malankara, and Eritrean belong inside Oriental Orthodox.

When a user selects a tradition-family where current coverage is partial, show a threshold information screen before routing to the available governed form. Phrase coverage as 'This release begins with...' rather than an error or apology. Optional email signup may be offered only as scoped pastoral service, not marketing.
architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.notification_rule: Tradition-expansion notification must be opt-in, visually secondary, scoped to the selected tradition-family, and not treated as a general mailing-list funnel unless the user separately chooses broader updates.

architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.decision: The shared app architecture should remain coherent across traditions, but each tradition may carry a subtle visual register rooted in its own prayer, book culture, calendar, terminology, and ecclesial memory. TEC may feel like a sober American prayer-book tradition; the Agpeya may gently remember Coptic Christianity; Byzantine, East Syriac, Ethiopian, and other lanes may receive similarly restrained registers. No tradition may be reduced to a costume skin or visual theme pack.
architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.design_formula: One app. One architecture. Many received rooms.

architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.decision: Universal Office will treat the Church of the East lane as an East Syriac Office using Hudra/Khudra as an acceptable broad public-facing label where appropriate, not as a claim that every rendered component comes narrowly from one liturgical book. Cathedral and Monastic are distinct usage models: Cathedral represents living public/parish practice and may be curated to Ramsha, Lelya, and Sapra when supported by practice/source evidence; Monastic represents the fuller canonical hour cycle and may expose Quta'a, Endana, D-tsha' Sa'in / Ninth Hour, Suba'a, and other implemented hours as full-cycle prayer rather than ordinary cathedral default.

governance.shheemo_term_policy: Sh'heemo/Shehimo is West Syriac and must not be used as the East Syriac/Church of the East lane label.
governance.practice_witness_policy: Community reports, parish descriptions, Reddit discussions, and similar resources may guide usage-mode decisions, cathedral/monastic defaults, and UI emphasis, but they are not textual authorities.
governance.source_authority_policy: Actual office text, rubrics, psalmody, readings, responsories, hymnody, and provenance remain governed by source-tracked liturgical books and corpus witnesses.
governance.comparative_witness_policy: West Syriac Fenqiotho/Fanqitho material may be used as a comparative Syriac liturgical witness for office idiom, feast-proper structure, and Semitic liturgical register, but it is not a direct Hudra/Endana source and must not determine East Syriac structure.

ui_implications.hudra_label_policy: Hudra / Khudra may remain as a broad recognizable label where source notes preserve internal book distinctions.
ui_implications.visibility_rule: Do not show all implemented East Syriac hours as cathedral-normal merely because they exist. Less common public hours should be marked as monastic, expanded, restored, or full-cycle when practice/source evidence does not support cathedral default use.

accepted_practice_leads: r/divineoffice East Syriac Office / Hudra discussion as practice witness only
accepted_comparative_leads: ephremishac/syriac-liturgy Fenqiotho material as West Syriac comparative witness only
blocked_claims: Sh'heemo is an East Syriac label
Fenqiotho is the West Syriac Hudra in a one-to-one sense
Reddit or community practice reports authorize office text
All implemented East Syriac hours are automatically cathedral-default
Hudra broad-label usage removes the need to track Qdham u-Wathar, Psalter/Hullale, Gezza, Kashkol, Bedjan, Darmo, or other source-family distinctions

architectural_decisions_2026_05_25.decision: Universal Office will distinguish canonical office families from auxiliary or companion office-shaped devotions. The main tradition selector is reserved for governed ecclesial office families such as Anglican Daily Office, Roman LOTH / Roman Breviary, Byzantine Horologion, Ethiopian Sa’atat, East Syriac Office, Coptic Agpeya, West Syriac Shehimo, and other full office systems. Little offices, order-specific offices, votive/proper offices, psalter devotions, manuscript devotional cycles, novenas, rosaries, adoration cycles, and similar prayer forms may be researched and eventually surfaced, but they must not be flattened into main office-family lanes.

governance.community_research_policy: Community lists, Reddit threads, blog indexes, archive pointers, and similar resources may seed research registries and product-intelligence questions, but they do not authorize liturgical text, office structure, rubrics, or public rendering.
governance.source_authority_policy: Actual office text, rubrics, psalmody, readings, hymns, collects, calendars, and provenance must remain governed by identifiable liturgical books, manuscripts, public-domain sources, licensed sources, or explicitly recorded internal/private source witnesses.
governance.taxonomy_policy: Every non-primary office-shaped item must be typed before implementation. Do not build an item merely because it resembles an office or appears in a devotional list.
governance.selector_policy: The main tradition selector must not become a catalog of every office-shaped devotion. Auxiliary material belongs behind a secondary Companion Offices / Devotions layer once governed.
governance.rights_policy: Historical availability, archive visibility, or devotional popularity does not imply public-rendering clearance. Rights and source-status posture must be recorded separately from corpus construction and UI discovery.

canonical_office_family.definition: A full ecclesial daily-office system requiring calendar logic, office resolution, rubrics, diagnostics, source governance, and tradition-specific UI posture.
examples: East Syriac Office / Hudra broad-label lane

order_or_rite_office.definition: A governed office use belonging to a religious order, rite, monastic usage, or historically distinct liturgical family.

little_office.definition: An office-shaped companion devotion with hour-like structure, often semi-static or lightly variable, normally adjacent to rather than replacing the appointed daily office.
examples: Little Office of the Blessed Virgin Mary
Little Office of the Holy Cross
Little Office of the Holy Trinity
Little Office of the Blessed Sacrament
Little Office of the Holy Angels

proper_or_votive_office.definition: An office appointed for a particular liturgical condition, saint, dead, feast, votive observance, or special use.
examples: Saturday Office of the Blessed Virgin Mary

psalter_devotion.definition: A structured psalm or psalm-like devotional sequence that may resemble the office but is not itself a full daily-office family.

devotional_cycle.definition: A repeated prayer structure such as a rosary, novena, adoration cycle, or time-bound devotion. These may be valuable but must not be mislabeled as canonical offices unless the source tradition explicitly does so.

source_lead_only.definition: A mention, link, archive listing, Reddit item, blog pointer, storefront entry, or unverified PDF lead that has not yet been governed as usable source material.

engine_implications.canonical_office_families: Use tradition-specific resolver engines with calendar, season, proper, source, and diagnostics boundaries.
engine_implications.companion_offices: Future implementation should use a shared Companion Office engine with typed slots such as opening versicle, hymn, antiphon, psalm_or_canticle, reading_or_chapter, responsory_or_versicle, collect, and conclusion.
engine_implications.devotional_cycles: Future implementation should use a separate Devotional Cycle engine rather than borrowing office-family resolver semantics.
engine_implications.no_broad_import: Do not bulk-import enormous lists of little offices or devotions. Add research registry entries first, then source-governed prototypes.

ui_implications.secondary_action: Add a companion office or devotion.
ui_implications.visibility_rule: A prayer form may be visible as research, planned, or unavailable without becoming selectable for prayer. Public prayer rendering requires source, rights, and structure governance.

high_value: Little Office of the Blessed Virgin Mary
Hours of the Holy Cross / Compassion
medium_value: Little Office of the Holy Trinity
Little Office of the Blessed Sacrament
Little Office of the Sacred Heart
Little Office of the Holy Angels
All Saints and order-specific saint offices
defer_or_research_only: modern boutique offices without clear ecclesial/source pedigree

blocked_claims: Every office-shaped devotion belongs in the main office selector
A devotional cycle is a canonical office because it has hours or repeated sections
Little offices should be implemented through full tradition engines by default
Universal Office should become an undifferentiated catalog of all known offices and devotions

decisions.title: Byzantine Tradition Profile Governance
decisions.summary: The Universal Office Horologion is governed as multi-tradition capable. The first fully implemented recension remains Slavic Byzantine, but the architecture is designed to support additional Byzantine tradition profiles (e.g. Antiochian, Greek) through profile-specific corpora and rubrical overlays.
details: Office structure and resolver architecture remain common across traditions wherever possible.
Textual corpora such as Octoechos, Menaion, and other hymnographic collections may be tradition-profile specific.
Future tradition profiles should be implemented through data overlays rather than separate engines.

decisions.title: Octoechos Corpus Namespace Standard
decisions.summary: Octoechos hymnographic corpora are normalized under a single namespace keyed by office → family → case → tone.
details: Octoechos data should live under window.OCTOECHOS.
Recommended access pattern: window.OCTOECHOS.<office>.<family>.<case>.tones.
Example: window.OCTOECHOS.orthros.praises.sunday.tones.
Repetitive hymnographic items may be stored as compact string arrays and expanded into structured objects by the resolver at runtime.

decisions.title: Octoechos Tone-of-Week Computation
decisions.summary: The Octoechos tone of the week is computed algorithmically from Pascha rather than stored by date in corpus files.
details: The weekly tone cycle begins with Tone 1 on Thomas Sunday and advances by one each week, cycling 1–8.
Bright Week suspends the ordinary Octoechos weekly cycle.
Corpus files store hymnography by tone, not by civil date.
Seasonal and festal layers may override tone-governed hymnography, but they do not replace the anchor calculation.

decisions.title: Liturgical Education Layer
decisions.summary: The Universal Office is governed as both a devotional tool and a pedagogical tool. Educational explanation is a first-class architectural layer, not incidental UI ornament.
details: Each prayer tradition should support educational explanation appropriate to its own structure and terminology.
Educational content should distinguish terminology, office structure, and tradition-specific practice.
The system should support layered explanation: micro-explanation, structural explanation, and tradition explanation.
Educational notes must clarify differences between traditions without flattening them.

decisions.title: Octoechos Base Corpus + Tradition Overlay Model
decisions.summary: Octoechos hymnographic corpora are to be governed by a shared base corpus with tradition-profile overlays rather than duplicated full corpora wherever textual divergence is limited.
details: Shared Byzantine material should live in a base corpus namespace.
Tradition-specific differences (for example Slavic, Greek, Antiochian) should be represented as overlays whenever possible.
Resolver logic should merge a selected tradition overlay onto the shared base corpus at runtime.
Full separate family corpora should be used only when divergence is too large for overlay-based maintenance to remain coherent.
This governance rule applies especially to Orthros hymnographic families such as Praises, Exapostilarion, Sessional Hymns, and Canon.

decisions.title: Byzantine Horologion — Monastic-First Default and Retired Pentecost Gate
decisions.summary: The Byzantine Horologion remains governed as monastic-first by default. Western Pentecost 2026 is retired as an active release target and preserved as the prior aspirational gate. Current release governance is post-Pentecost stabilization and public-beta readiness gating; EO production-path integration is complete as of 2026-05-16.
details: Monastic-first means the full daily cycle as observed in monastic typika (Great Compline, Midnight Office, Orthros, Hours, Typika, Vespers) is the governing completeness target, not the secular or cathedral parish subset.

Great Compline (Mega Apodeipnon) baseline is already live: the office is structurally distinct from Small Compline and remains release-critical in the broader monastic completeness sense, but its remaining work is corpus expansion and refinement rather than baseline implementation.

Typika (Obednitsa) baseline is already live: Sunday resurrectional kontakion layer (tones 1–8) is complete; dismissal is rubric-only by present design; remaining work is lectionary infrastructure rather than baseline implementation.
Interhours (Mezhduchasiye — First, Third, Sixth, Ninth) baseline is already live: remaining work is fixed-text deepening rather than baseline implementation.
The immediate governed Byzantine task is now the monastic audit: festal/seasonal replacement logic, kathisma depth, and remaining rubric-only pressure points across already-live offices.

EO main-app integration (eo-calendar-modes todo) is complete as of 2026-05-16: js/calendar-eastern-orthodox.js is loaded on the main-app production path, the Horologion sidebar exposes the EO calendar mode selector, and HorologionEngine.resolveOffice() consumes eoMode for fixed-date lookups. Remaining EO calendar governance concerns annual Typikon collision policy for fixed/movable overlaps and is deferred separately.

The former Western Pentecost 2026 release gate is retired as an active target. Current readiness is defined as: honest monastic structural baseline in hand, release-honesty defects closed or explicitly deferred, EO production-path integration completed, and public-beta blockers stated plainly.

decisions.decision: The prior broad Office of Readings daily date-block population model is retired from active corpus. The Office of Readings must be built as reusable cycle/proper/resolver architecture with unitRefs and source-class-separated source-pipeline fixtures. Universalis may govern appointment and structure discovery; local Grail governs psalm-body materialization. Broad resolved daily snapshots are prohibited by guard audit unless a future explicit governance decision reverses this.

critical_path_offices.title: Typika (Obednitsa)
critical_path_offices.note: Baseline architecture is live. Sunday resurrectional kontakion layer (tones 1–8) is complete. Ordinary post-Pentecost Sunday epistle/gospel infrastructure is live for SAP Weeks 1–31; Week 1 All Saints epistle and gospel both render full text as of 2026-05-07 (epistle via epistle_segments Hebrews 11:32–40 + 12:1–2; gospel via segmented references Matthew 10:32-33, 10:37-38, 19:27-30). Pentecostarion Sunday readings, active pre-Lenten Sunday readings, and Great Lent Sunday readings are live. Typika Epistle/Gospel scripture resolution is now helper-normalized via resolveScripturePericope() as of 2026-05-10. Palm Sunday and Bright Week readings are live as of 2026-05-10. Ordinary weekday readings after Pentecost Weeks 1-33 are live as split-key weekday support as of 2026-05-10: Weeks 1-17 provide pre-Lukan ordinary Epistle/Gospel fallback, Weeks 18-33 provide post-Lukan Epistle support, and post-Lukan Gospel Weeks 30-33 are live in post_lukan_weekday_gospels. Lukan weekday Gospel Weeks 1-12 are live as of 2026-05-10. Typika weekday interruption gates are live for Nativity/Theophany and Triodion/pre-Lenten stop conditions. Post-Theophany weekday realignment is live using countdown keying toward Publican and Pharisee Sunday, so Week 33 resolves correctly after Zacchaeus Sunday even when raw Sunday-after-Pentecost ordinals differ. Typika pre-Lenten weekday readings are live through Cheesefare Saturday in pre_lenten_weekdays, including no-Divine-Liturgy handling for Cheesefare Wednesday and Friday and multi-reading-set handling for Meatfare Saturday of Souls and Cheesefare Saturday Holy Ascetics. General feast lectionary overlays are live with feast_lectionary_overlays.winter_cycle populated for core Nativity/Theophany fixed days, relative Sundays, and relative Saturdays; feast_lectionary_overlays.fixed_cycle is populated for low/moderate-risk major fixed feasts; guarded Annunciation support is live for non-Sunday March 25 dates before Lazarus Saturday. Collision append/combine behavior and overlay-first fallback remain active. Final Typika release regression is closed as of 2026-05-10. Remaining Typika work is deferred and non-release-blocking: Annunciation Sunday/Holy Week/Pascha/Bright collision policy, broader Menaion/fixed-feast overlay expansion, fuller forefeast/afterfeast coverage if later required, Royal Hours/office-reading handling, and other special-cycle lectionary expansion; dismissal is rubric-only by present design, not an open depth item.

liturgical_research_governance.description: Durable liturgical research and governance decisions affecting Universal Office structure, source lanes, office-family sequencing, and future resolver design.

entries.topic: West Syriac Shehimo office family and Eastern Catholic subtradition sequencing

research_summary.office_family: West Syriac / Syro-Antiochene Shehimo, also rendered Shhimo or Shhimo.
research_summary.core_finding: The Shehimo is the ordinary West Syriac daily office / canonical hours tradition. It is a liturgical office family, not an ecclesial communion label.
research_summary.important_distinction: West Syriac is not synonymous with Eastern Catholic. The same West Syriac office family is received by Oriental Orthodox, Eastern Catholic, independent Malankara, and reformed Malankara / Anglican-influenced bodies.

oriental_orthodox_receptions: Jacobite Syrian Christian / Malankara Jacobite Shehimo
eastern_catholic_receptions: Syro-Malankara Catholic West Syriac Shehimo / Divine Office reception
Syriac Catholic West Syriac Shehimo reception
related_but_distinct: Maronite office tradition is West-Syriac-related but should not be collapsed into the ordinary Shehimo lane without separate source and resolver review.
Mar Thoma daily prayer belongs to the reformed Malankara / West Syriac-derived ecology and is Anglican-influenced in reception, but is not an Eastern Catholic lane.

governance_decisions.decision: Agpeya remains the next intended Oriental Orthodox office-family buildout.
West Syriac Shehimo is accepted as a future office family after the Agpeya buildout or later, depending on release priorities and source readiness.

The first West Syriac implementation should prioritize an Oriental Orthodox source lane, likely Syriac Orthodox / Malankara Orthodox / Jacobite, using a strong English source with clear source-governance posture.
West Syriac Shehimo — Syro-Malankara Catholic edition is valid as an Eastern Catholic reception but is deferred until after gated beta and public release.
West Syriac Shehimo — Syriac Catholic edition is valid as an Eastern Catholic reception but is deferred until after gated beta and public release.

Do not collapse Oriental Orthodox and Eastern Catholic West Syriac editions into a single undifferentiated text lane. Shared rite-family logic may be reusable, but source lanes, calendars, commemorative layers, permissions, and edition authority must remain distinct.

architecture_implications.recommended_model: One West Syriac Shehimo office-family resolver family may eventually support multiple source-governed edition lanes.
architecture_implications.resolver_warning: Shared hour structure does not imply identical text, calendar, saints, commemorations, rubrics, or devotional appendices.
architecture_implications.source_warning: A lay prayer book, abridged devotional edition, or Catholic-approved prayer manual must not be treated automatically as equivalent to the full Orthodox Shehimo.
source_governance_required_before_build: Record ecclesial authority and edition scope.
Distinguish full office, parish/lay abridgment, devotional supplement, and calendar material.
Record permissions/public-rendering posture separately from private corpus construction posture.
Add resolver diagnostics for missing hours, abridged material, and edition-specific omissions.

near_term: Coptic Orthodox Agpeya remains the next Oriental Orthodox buildout candidate.
medium_term: West Syriac Shehimo Oriental Orthodox edition may become the next Oriental-family office after Agpeya.
late_expansion: Syro-Malankara Catholic Shehimo / Divine Office edition.
not_current_work: Do not begin Eastern Catholic West Syriac corpus work during gated beta unless Josh explicitly reopens release scope.
Do not use governance-only placeholder work as a substitute for source-backed corpus work when an implementation tranche begins.
